questions.q: Explain the concept of rural poverty and its causes. (a) Distinguish between chronic and transitory poverty. (b) What are the consequences of rural poverty?
questions.answer: Rural poverty refers to the condition of a section of the rural population that fails to meet their basic needs. The causes of rural poverty are multifaceted and include (a) low agricultural productivity, (b) lack of access to education and healthcare, (c) limited employment opportunities, and (d) inadequate infrastructure. Chronic poverty is a long-term phenomenon where individuals and families are unable to escape poverty, whereas transitory poverty is a short-term condition where individuals and families experience poverty due to various shocks such as crop failure or illness. The consequences of rural poverty are far-reaching and include (a) low levels of economic growth, (b) increased inequality, and (c) social unrest.
marking_scheme: Explain the concept of rural poverty (1 mark)
Distinguish between chronic and transitory poverty (1.5 marks)

questions.q: Discuss the role of rural infrastructure in rural development. (a) What are the key components of rural infrastructure? (b) How do they contribute to rural development?
questions.answer: Rural infrastructure plays a vital role in rural development by (a) improving access to markets, (b) enhancing agricultural productivity, and (c) providing basic services such as healthcare and education. The key components of rural infrastructure include (a) roads, (b) irrigation systems, (c) storage facilities, and (d) communication networks. These components contribute to rural development by (a) increasing economic opportunities, (b) improving the quality of life, and (c) reducing poverty.
marking_scheme: Key components of rural infrastructure (1.5 marks)
Contribution of rural infrastructure to rural development (3 marks)

questions.q: Explain the concept of rural credit and its importance in rural development. (a) What are the types of rural credit? (b) How do they benefit rural households?
questions.answer: Rural credit refers to the provision of financial services to rural households and enterprises. It is essential for rural development as it (a) enables households to invest in productive activities, (b) provides liquidity during times of need, and (c) promotes economic growth. The types of rural credit include (a) production credit, (b) consumption credit, and (c) investment credit. These types of credit benefit rural households by (a) increasing their income, (b) improving their quality of life, and (c) reducing poverty.
marking_scheme: Importance of rural credit in rural development (3 marks)

questions.q: Discuss the role of rural cooperatives in rural development. (a) What are the types of rural cooperatives? (b) How do they contribute to rural development?
questions.answer: Rural cooperatives play a vital role in rural development by (a) promoting economic growth, (b) improving access to markets, and (c) enhancing the bargaining power of rural households. The types of rural cooperatives include (a) agricultural cooperatives, (b) credit cooperatives, and (c) consumers' cooperatives. These types of cooperatives contribute to rural development by (a) increasing the income of rural households, (b) improving their quality of life, and (c) reducing poverty.
marking_scheme: Contribution of rural cooperatives to rural development (3 marks)

questions.q: Explain the concept of rural marketing and its importance in rural development. (a) What are the characteristics of rural markets? (b) How do they cater to the needs of rural households?
questions.answer: Rural marketing refers to the process of promoting and selling goods and services to rural households and enterprises. It is essential for rural development as it (a) improves access to markets, (b) increases the income of rural households, and (c) enhances the quality of life. The characteristics of rural markets include (a) limited access to information, (b) low levels of literacy, and (c) limited economic resources. Rural marketing caters to the needs of rural households by (a) providing essential goods and services, (b) promoting economic growth, and (c) improving their quality of life.
marking_scheme: Importance of rural marketing in rural development (3 marks)
